hero.title: Documentation
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to use Another Agent Skills.

overview.title: Overview
overview.welcome: Another Agent Skills is a framework of 41 composable skills that turn AI coding agents into disciplined senior engineers.
overview.gettingStartedDesc: Installation, setup, and first project walkthrough.
overview.lifecycleDesc: The 6-phase development flow: Define, Plan, Build, Verify, Review, Ship.
overview.skillsDesc: Browse all 41 skills with filtering by category and lifecycle phase.
overview.enforcementDesc: Mechanical enforcement: manifest gate, tokens, pre-commit hooks.
overview.philosophyDesc: SOUL.md principles, honest limitations, and what we never do.
overview.agentsDesc: Compatible agents: OpenCode, Claude Code, Cursor, Devin Desktop, and more.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.prereqAgent: OpenCode recommended, or any compatible agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Devin Desktop, Kiro)
gettingStarted.newProjectDesc: Start from scratch in an empty directory:
gettingStarted.nextStepsSummary: After running, you'll see a "Next Steps" summary:
gettingStarted.existingProjectDesc: If you already have code and want to add skill-driven enforcement:
gettingStarted.existingStep4: Detects your stack and creates STACK_CONFIG.md if missing
gettingStarted.stackDetectionDesc: init-agents automatically detects your project stack from lockfiles and config files:
gettingStarted.stackCreates: It creates STACK_CONFIG.md with your actual commands (test, lint, build, dev). All skills read from this file.
gettingStarted.openCodeDesc: No extra setup. Skills load automatically via the skill tool.
gettingStarted.claudeCodeDesc: Creates .claude-plugin/agent-discipline/ with hooks. To also load SOUL.md and AGENTS.md rules, copy the key principles into your CLAUDE.md.
gettingStarted.devinDesktopDesc: Creates .kiro/hooks/agent-discipline.json with pre-flight, commit approval, and edit guard hooks.
gettingStarted.anyGitAgentDesc: The git hooks work with any agent that uses git. Copy the hooks to your project:

lifecycle.title: The 6-Phase Lifecycle
lifecycle.subtitle: Every project follows the same disciplined flow. No shortcuts. No skipped steps.
lifecycle.phase1Goal: Write the spec before any code.
lifecycle.phase3Goal: Implement one slice at a time.
lifecycle.phase4Goal: Run tests. Check builds. Verify behavior.
lifecycle.phase4ToolGapDesc: If tools can't reach the world, report "ship status unknown." Never fake a win.
lifecycle.purposeDesc: The lifecycle weights phases by session purpose:

skills.title: Skills Catalog
skills.subtitle: 41 composable skills. Each one handles a specific engineering discipline.

enforcement.title: Enforcement
enforcement.subtitle: Mechanical enforcement, not suggestions.
enforcement.level1Desc: Agent remembers rules. Load-bearing for simple tasks.
enforcement.level2Desc: Agent presents DECISION POINT before every mutation.
enforcement.level3Desc: Hook verifies integrity. Agent cannot bypass what it cannot forge.
enforcement.level4Desc: Agent cannot generate tokens without writing a commit manifest first.
enforcement.honestDesc: These gates create friction, not guarantees. The human must stay alert.

philosophy.title: Philosophy
philosophy.subtitle: SOUL.md principles and what we never do.
philosophy.principle1: Quality emerges from explicit contracts, not implicit trust.
philosophy.principle2: Rules that depend on memory fail. Rules that depend on visible blocks succeed.
philosophy.principle3: A hook that accepts any token is a suggestion. A hook that verifies integrity is a gate.
philosophy.principle4: Speed is a side effect of precision, never the primary goal.
philosophy.principle5: Context is finite. Evict before you drown.
philosophy.principle6: No, that's overcomplicated is more valuable than blind compliance.
philosophy.principle7: We learn from our own failures faster than competitors ship features.
philosophy.honestDesc: These gates create friction, not guarantees. The human must stay alert.

agents.title: Agent Compatibility
agents.subtitle: Git hooks work everywhere. Skill concepts load automatically for OpenCode.
